Modal.wrapper: fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm
Modal.content: relative w-full max-w-5xl bg-white rounded-xl shadow-2xl overflow-hidden max-h-[90vh] flex flex-col
Modal.header: px-8 py-6 border-b border-slate-100 flex justify-between items-center bg-white sticky top-0 z-10

ScoreGauge.container: relative flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-6 bg-white rounded-xl border border-slate-100 shadow-sm

StatusBadge.base: inline-flex items-center px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium

DataCard.wrapper: bg-white p-5 rounded-xl border border-slate-100 shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-200
DataCard.label: text-xs font-medium text-slate-500 uppercase tracking-wider mb-1

assets.icons: Use Lucide React icons (ShieldCheck, AlertCircle, CreditCard, History, TrendingUp, User, Calendar, Mail, Phone)
assets.logos: Use text labels 'Experian' and 'Equifax' with distinct brand colors (Blue #004691 for Experian, Red #D40000 for Equifax) if logos are unavailable.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Create a new component `CreditCheckModal.js` in `/app/frontend/src/components/loans/` (create directory if needed).
2. Use `framer-motion` for smooth transitions between steps (Form -> OTP -> Report).
3. Implement a custom SVG Gauge for the credit score. Do not use a heavy charting library just for this.
4. Ensure the 'Report' view is dense but readable. Use the 'Bento' grid layout for summary metrics.
6. Mock the API calls with `setTimeout` to simulate loading states (Form submission -> 2s -> OTP sent).
7. For the Credit Report data, create a robust mock JSON object that includes both Experian and Equifax data structures.
8. The 'Account List' should be a clean table with status badges (Active, Closed, Default).
9. Add a 'Download PDF' button in the report header (mock functionality).
10. Ensure all inputs have `data-testid` attributes for testing.
universal_guidelines: Avoid generic 'Bootstrap' looks. Use custom padding and shadows.
Use 'Inter' font with 'tnum' (tabular nums) for all financial data.
Ensure high contrast for all text (WCAG AA compliance).
Do not use pure black (#000000). Use Slate-900 (#0F172A) instead.
